name: Joe Morello
abstract: Joseph Albert Morello (July 17, 1928 – March 12, 2011) was an American jazz drummer best known for serving as the drummer for pianist Dave Brubeck, as part of the Dave Brubeck Quartet, from 1957 to 1972, including during the quartet's "classic lineup" from 1958 to 1968, which also included alto saxophonist Paul Desmond and bassist Eugene…

about_quotes.speaker: Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Joe Morello's role in the Dave Brubeck Quartet and his relationship with Dave Brubeck and Paul Desmond, but never claims a personal connection or direct interaction with Joe Morello himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Tell me about Joe Morello coming into the quartet... So I talked to Joe... Our next and first job was Chicago at the Blue Note... So I was able to save my group... I could see where Joe and I were going to go.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And then my main trio after a couple of months was Bill Crow and Joe Morello. And I mean that was the main trio for about three or four years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Morello was our drummer who uh I think everybody knows who Joe Morello was but in case you were asleep he played with Dave Brubeck and did the first drum solo in “Take Five,” Paul Desmond’s song. Um and Chuck Andrus was on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: So the next year I made a record, playing it safe this time, with just a trio, with Gene Chirico on bass, who I knew well, and Joe Morello, my old friend, on drums. That was New Vibe Man In Town.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I had a few different guys and finally arrived at this good trio with Bill Crow and Joe Morello. That was the . . . one of the good groups I had in there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
